content.problemStatement:
There is a cricket match in Chefland. Chef's team requires NNN runs to win in MMM overs.

Given that 111 over consists of 666 balls and a player can score a maximum of 666 runs in a ball, find whether Chef's team can win.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of runs required to win the game and the remaining number of overs.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, `YES`, if Chef's team can win the game. Otherwise, output `NO`.


You can print each character in uppercase or lowercase. For example `NO`, `no`, `No`, and `nO` are all considered identical.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and M — the runs required and the remaining overs.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if Chef's team can reach at least N runs in the remaining overs, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we compute the maximum possible runs (M*36) and compare with N. 500 ≤ 720 → YES, 100 > 72 → NO, 30 ≤ 36 → YES, 216 = 216 → YES.

solution.approach: Each over has 6 balls and each ball can yield at most 6 runs, so the maximum runs possible in M overs is M * 6 * 6 = 36 * M. For each test case we compare N with 36*M. If N <= 36*M the answer is YES, otherwise NO. This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
